In the analysis of dermatologic manifestations, the table reveals that for nonspecific erythema, the frequency in the exposure
negative group is 1, while in the exposure positive group it is 5; for rosacea acnea, the values are 1 and 2, respectively; for
erythema, scaling, the frequencies are 1 and 3; for itching, burning, the exposure negative group shows 2 and the positive group
shows 17; thus, the sum totals 5 for the exposure negative group and 27 for the exposure positive group. Furthermore, in the
assessment of wheat and milk proteins, the wheat lgG shows values of 0.01720.7314 for GAD-65 lgG, -0.00780.8772 for
cerebellar lgG, 0.10250.0404 Significant for MBP lgG, and -0.00290.9534 for MOG lgG. The α-gliadin 33-mer lgG presents
0.11160.0256 Significant for GAD-65 lgG, -0.05760.2500 for cerebellar lgG, 0.14560.0035 Significant for MBP lgG, and
-0.00240.9623 for MOG lgG. The γ-gliadin lgG values are 0.12510.0123 Significant for GAD-65 lgG, 0.03230.5193 for
cerebellar lgG, 0.14790.0030 Significant for MBP lgG, and 0.00510.9192 for MOG lgG. The cow’s milk lgG yields results of
0.09320.0626 for GAD-65 lgG, 0.09110.0689 for cerebellar lgG, 0.05950.2350 for MBP lgG, and 0.05250.2949 for MOG lgG.
The α + β-casein lgG shows values of 0.07630.1280 for GAD-65 lgG, 0.04320.3897 for cerebellar lgG, 0.08470.0912 for MBP
lgG, and -0.07600.1296 for MOG lgG. Lastly, the milk butyrophilin lgG indicates results of 0.05060.3128 for GAD-65 lgG,
0.2680<0.0001 Significant for cerebellar lgG, 0.16420.0010 Significant for MBP lgG, and 0.16180.0012 Significant for MOG
lgG.
